sections.heading: Primary cutaneous B-cell lymphomas
sections.content: Molecular pathogenesis varies significantly by subtype. In primary cutaneous B-cell lymphomas: PCFCL is characterized by a germinal center B-cell (GCB) phenotype and typically lacks MYD88 and CD79B mutations. PCMZL can be divided by Ig heavy chain usage (Ig class-switched vs. IgM+) with different prognoses. PCDLBCL, leg type is characterized by an activated B-cell (ABC) phenotype and the MCD subtype, with high prevalence of gain-of-function mutations in MYD88 L265P and CD79B. Inactivation of CDKN2A and MYD88 L265P mutations are associated with inferior prognosis in this subtype.

sections.heading: Cutaneous T-cell lymphomas
sections.content: In cutaneous T-cell lymphomas: MF and SS are driven by the clonal proliferation of skin-resident (MF) or thymic memory (SS) T cells. Large-cell transformation (LCT) is defined by >25% large cells. In primary cutaneous CD30+ T-cell LPDs, DUSP22-IRF4 gene rearrangement has been described in PC-ALCL and LyP but is not prognostic. A subset of CTCL may carry activating JAK2 rearrangements. In SPTCL, germline mutations in HAVCR2 (encoding TIM-3) are frequent (25%-85%) and are associated with HLH and may be more prevalent in patients of Asian ancestry. Recurrent mutations in epigenetic modifiers (KMT2C, KMT2D) and the PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway (PLCG1, ARID1B) have also been identified. The absence of common lymphoma driver mutations in SPTCL suggests an immune dysregulation process.
